sarga.title: Rama, Lakshmana and Sita take leave of Atri and Anasuya -- enter the Dandaka forest.

shlokas.original:
अनसूया तु धर्मज्ञा श्रुत्वा तां महतीं कथाम्।
पर्यष्वजत बाहुभ्यां शिरस्याघ्राय मैथिलीम्।।2.119.1।।
shlokas.translation: Then righteous Anasuya, having heard the great story of Sita, kissed her on the forehead and embraced her with both the arms.

shlokas.original:
व्यक्ताक्षरपदं चित्रं भाषितं मधुरं त्वया।
यथा स्वयंवरं वृत्तं तत्सर्वं हि श्रुतं मया।।2.119.2।।
रमेऽहं कथया ते तु दृढं मधुरभाषिणि।
shlokas.translation: You have related wonderfully and sweetly your Swayamvara with each word and each syllable uttered clearly. I have heard everything as it happened. O sweetspeaking Sita, I have greatly enjoyed your story.

shlokas.original:
रविरस्तङ्गतश्श्रीमानुपोह्य रजनीं शिवाम्।।2.119.3।।
दिवसं प्रतिकीर्णानामाहारार्थं पतत्रिणाम्।
सन्ध्याकाले निलीनानां निद्रार्थं श्रूयते ध्वनिः।।2.119.4।।
shlokas.translation: The resplendent Sun has set. The auspcious night is drawing near. The birds who forage far and wide for food during the day return to their nests to rest for the night. You can hear them twittering.

shlokas.original:
एते चाप्यभिषेकार्द्रा मुनयः कलशोद्यताः।
सहिता उपवर्तन्ते सलिलाप्लुतवल्कलाः।।2.119.5।।
shlokas.translation: The sages also  are returning in groups with waterpitchers filled with water and their bark robes soaked with water after their ceremonial bath.

shlokas.original:
ऋषीणामग्निहोत्रेषु हुतेषु विधिपूर्वकम्।
कपोताङ्गारुणो धूमो दृश्यते पवनोद्धतः।।2.119.6।।
shlokas.translation: The ascetics have duly offered oblations to the fire in Agnihotra sacrifices and the smoke, tawny like the pigeon's body, emanates wafted by the wind.

shlokas.original:
अल्पपर्णा हि तरवो घनीभूतास्समन्ततः।
विप्रकृष्टेन्द्रिये देशेऽस्मिन्न प्रकाशन्ति वै दिशः।।2.119.7।।
shlokas.translation: The trees with sparse leaves all over have grown dense, the quarters in the distant region are no longer discernible by the senses.

shlokas.original:
रजनीचरसत्त्वानि प्रचरन्ति समन्ततः।
तपोवनमृगा ह्येते वेदितीर्थेषु शेरते।।2.119.8।।
shlokas.translation: The night rangers have started moving everywhere and the deer in the penance  grove are beginning to sleep around the sacred altars.

shlokas.original:
सम्प्रवृत्तानिशा सीते नक्��त्रसमलङ्कृता।
ज्योत्स्नाप्रावरणश्चन्द्रो दृश्यतेऽभ्युदितोऽम्बरे।।2.119.9।।
shlokas.translation: O Sita, adorned with stars, the night has commenced. You can see the moon rising in the sky spreading his mantle of light.

shlokas.original:
गम्यतामनुजानामि रामस्यानुचरी भव।
कथायन्त्या हि मधुरं त्वयाऽहं परितोषिता।।2.119.10।।
shlokas.translation: With my permission you may go now and be a companion of Rama. I am thoroughly contented  with your story told so sweetly.

shlokas.original:
अलङ्कुरु च तावत्त्वं प्रत्यक्षं मम मैथिलि।
प्रीतिं जनय मे वत्से दिव्यालङ्कारशोभिता।।2.119.11।।
shlokas.translation: O Sita, adorn yourself in my presence. Dear child, permit me the pleasure of  adorning  you with these divine ornaments.

shlokas.original:
सा तथा समलङ्कृत्य सीता सुरसुतोपमा।
प्रणम्य शिरसा तस्यै रामं त्वभिमुखी ययौ।।2.119.12।।
shlokas.translation: Sita, adorned, looked like the daughter of a god. She bowed at the feet of Anasuya in reverence and set out to meet Rama.

shlokas.original:
तथा तु भूषितां सीतां ददर्श वदतां वरः।
राघवः प्रीतिदानेन तपस्विन्या जहर्ष च।।2.119.13।।
shlokas.translation: Rama, the most eloquent among men, saw Sita adorned, and rejoiced at the gifts of love given by the ascetic Anasuya.

shlokas.original:
न्यवेदयत्ततस्सर्वं सीता रामाय मैथिली।
प्रीतिदानं तपस्विन्या वसनाभरणस्रजम्।।2.119.14।।
shlokas.translation: Sita the princess of Mithila related everything to Rama and showed him the gifts of love given by ascetic Anasuya -- raiment, jewellery and garlands.

shlokas.original:
प्रहृष्टस्त्वभवद्रामो लक्ष्मणश्च महारथः।
मैथिल्यास्सत्क्रियां दृष्ट्वा मानुषेषु सुदुर्लभाम्।।2.119.15।।
shlokas.translation: Rama the great charioteer and Lakshmana were exceedingly gratified to see the honour conferred on Sita, rare among mortals.

shlokas.original:
ततस्तां शर्वरीं प्रीतः पुण्यां शशिनिभाननः।
अर्चितस्तापसै स्स���द्धैरुवास रघुनन्दनः।।2.119.16।।
shlokas.translation: Thereafter, the descendant of Raghu, whose countenance resembled the Moon, having received the hospitality of the accomplished ascetics, passed that holy night in delight.

shlokas.original:
तस्यां रात्र्यां व्यतीतायामभिषिच्य हुताग्निकान्।
आपृच्छेतां नरव्याघ्रौ तापसान्वनगोचरान्।।2.119.17।।
shlokas.translation: Night over, the two best men, Rama and Lakshmana, after their ablution took leave of the ascetics inhabiting the forest who offered morning oblations to the fire .

shlokas.original:
तावूचुस्ते वनचरास्तापसा धर्मचारिणः।
वनस्य तस्य सञ्चारं राक्षसैस्समभिप्लुतम्।।2.119.18।।
shlokas.translation: The righteous ascetics dwelling in the forest informed both Rama and Lakshmana  that  part of the forest infested by demons.

shlokas.original:
रक्षांसि पुरुषादानि नानारूपाणि राघव।
वसन्त्यस्मिन्महारण्ये व्यालाश्च रुधिराशनाः।।2.119.19।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama, this great forest is haunted by carnivorous demons who can assume any  form and by blooddrinking wild animals.

shlokas.original:
उच्छिष्टं वा प्रमत्तं वा तापसं धर्मचारिणम्।
अदन्त्यस्मिन्महारण्ये तान्निवारय राघव।।2.119.20।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama, in this great forest demons will devour the  ascetics if they find them impure or intoxicated or indolent. Prevent them.

shlokas.original:
एष पन्था महर्षीणां फलान्याहरतां वने।
अनेन तु वनं दुर्गं गन्तुं राघव ते क्षमम्।।2.119.21।।
shlokas.translation: O Rama, this is the one path in the forest used by the ascetics to gather fruits. You can safely cross the otherwise impassable forest through this path.

shlokas.original:
इतीव तैः प्राञ्जलिभिस्तपस्विभिर्द्विजैः कृतस्वस्त्ययनः परन्तपः।
वनं सभार्यः प्रविवेश राघवस्सलक्ष्मणस्सूर्य इवाभ्रमण्डलम्।।2.119.22।।
shlokas.translation: The ascetics offered their blessings to Rama the tormentor of enemies. Thereafter,  accompanied by his wife and Lakshmana, he entered the great forest like the Sun entering the mass of clouds.  इत्यार्षे श्रीमद्रामायणे श्रीमद्वाल्मीकीय आदिकाव्ये चतुर्विंशत्सहस्रिकायां संहितयां श्रीमदयोध्याकाण्डे एकोनविंशत्युत्तरशततमस्सर्गः।।    Thus ends the hundrednineteenth sarga in Ayodhyakanda of the holy Ramayana, the first epic composed by sage Valmiki.
